bug_report.summary: Unexpected extra gutter/marker (“Y”) appears next to YAML frontmatter line near 'keywords:'
steps_to_reproduce: Open the “Welcome to Zettlr!” document so the YAML frontmatter is visible.
Observe the gutter/left margin next to the 'keywords:' line.
expected_behaviors: No unexpected glyph/marker should appear in the gutter/left margin beyond normal line numbers/markers.
actual_behaviors: After the code change, an additional small 'Y' marker appears in the gutter area next to the YAML frontmatter (near the 'keywords:' line) that was not present before.

bug_report.summary: Closing the update dialog results in wrong/mismatched file selection highlight in the left file tree
steps_to_reproduce: Open the app to the tutorial workspace with multiple docs visible in the file tree (e.g., “Welcome to Zettlr!” and “Helpful Links”).
Ensure “Welcome to Zettlr!” is the active/open document (visible as the active tab/content).
Dismiss the update notification dialog (click “Not now”).
Observe the selection/highlight state in the left file tree.
expected_behaviors: Dismissing the update dialog should not change which document is selected/highlighted in the file tree.
The file tree’s highlighted item should correspond to the active/open editor tab/document.
actual_behaviors: After dismissing the dialog, the file tree highlight shifts to “Helpful Links” while “Welcome to Zettlr!” remains the active/open document, creating an inconsistent selection state.

bug_report.summary: Ctrl+F opens Find UI but unexpectedly selects text and pre-fills the query
steps_to_reproduce: Open a Markdown document in the editor.
Place the caret inside text (do not select text).
Press Ctrl+F to open the Find UI.
Observe the Find input field and the document selection state.
expected_behaviors: Ctrl+F should open the Find UI without changing the current caret/selection state.
The Find input should not be automatically populated unless there is an intentional selection-based feature (and even then should match consistent pre-change behavior).
actual_behaviors: After the code change, pressing Ctrl+F results in an automatic selection being present (status shows “5 selected …”) and the Find field is pre-filled with the selected text (“and then some trailing text.”), whereas before the change the Find field opened empty and without introducing a new selection.
